name: Emmy Award
desc: Outstanding Live Coverage of a Current News Story – Long Form, for 'Revolution in Egypt: President Mubarak Steps Down' (CNN team win)

name: Emmy Award
desc: For following Syrian/Iraqi refugees across Europe by foot, boat and train

name: Emmy Award
desc: Outstanding News Special — Return to Mosul, for coverage of the fall of ISIS in Iraq and Syria

name: Emmy Award
desc: ISIS/Iraq & Syria coverage (2nd of the trio), part of three Emmys won for the ISIS/Mosul reporting package

name: Emmy Award
desc: ISIS/Iraq & Syria coverage (3rd of the trio), part of three Emmys won for the ISIS/Mosul reporting package

name: Peabody Award
desc: Arab Spring — Egypt & Libya. Peabody for 'CNN's Reporting of the Arab Spring including Worldwide Coverage: Egypt — Wave of Discontent and Uprising in Libya'

name: Peabody Award
desc: Chibok Schoolgirls / Boko Haram. CNN team Peabody; cited Damon's 'high-risk field reporting' as making coverage 'comprehensive and indispensable'

name: Peabody Award
desc: Battle of Mosul — Trapped with Iraqi Forces. Awarded with photojournalist Brice Laine; cited for 'calm, clarity, and razor-sharp photography' showing the 'psychological burden of the ISIS reign of terror, especially on children'

name: George Foster Peabody Award
desc: Fall of ISIS in Iraq and Syria. The prestigious individual Peabody Award, separate from the team Peabody wins above

name: IRE Medal
desc: Investigative Reporters and Editors Medal for in-depth reporting on the 2012 Benghazi consulate attack and its aftermath; with photojournalist Sarmad Qaseera

name: IWMF Courage in Journalism Award
desc: International Women's Media Foundation; for frontline reporting from war zones over a decade

name: Edward R. Murrow Award
desc: Excellence in Video and Reporting: Hard News categories; for the Mosul trapped-in-a-house reporting with Brice Laine

name: Edward R. Murrow Award
desc: Excellence in Video and Reporting: Hard News categories; for the Mosul trapped-in-a-house reporting with Brice Laine

name: Gracie Award
desc: For refugee crisis coverage across Europe

name: Time Warner Richard Parsons Community Impact Award
desc: Voted on by Time Warner (CNN's parent company) employees

name: Time Warner Excellence in Service Award
desc: Companion award from the same Time Warner ceremony

name: James W. Foley Humanitarian Award
desc: Awarded at the Newseum, Washington D.C.; recognizes her reporting and INARA advocacy for war-affected children

name: World of Children Crisis Award
desc: For INARA's work providing medical care to children in conflict zones

name: Syrian-American Medical Society Humanitarian Award
desc: For INARA's work with Syrian children
